output_summary: profiled datasets: kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, yelp_small.
This run was an initial draft of the pipeline.
A complete restaurant entity resolution and fusion pipeline was created from scratch for kaggle_small, uber_eats_small, and yelp_small datasets.
The pipeline added LLM-based schema matching to align uber_eats_small and yelp_small columns to the kaggle_small schema before matching.
The pipeline implemented pairwise token blocking on the name_norm column for kaggle-to-Uber Eats, kaggle-to-Yelp, and Uber Eats-to-Yelp candidate generation.
The pipeline added rule-based entity matching for all three dataset pairs using the same weighted comparator configuration and a threshold of 0.7.
The pipeline concatenated all pairwise correspondences and added a data fusion stage with 19 attribute fusers before writing the fused output and pairwise correspondence files.
Fusion behavior was centered on a longest_string strategy, which was applied to 16 attributes as the dominant conflict resolution approach.
Matching behavior shifted to a name-led rule-based strategy in which name_norm carried the highest weight at 0.5, with supporting evidence from street, house_number, city, and categories.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run created an initial draft of the evaluation script for the fused restaurant output.
The dominant strategy was to align fused clusters to gold record IDs through parsed fusion-source mappings and then evaluate fields with type-specific functions selected by attribute semantics.
The evaluation used tokenized matching for text-like attributes, numeric tolerance matching for numeric attributes, and set equality matching for list-like attributes.
Name, website, map URL, phone, address, and location text fields were grouped under tokenized_match evaluation.
House number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match evaluation.
Categories and any detected list-like columns were grouped under set_equality_match evaluation after shared normalization across fused and gold data.
The run added helper functions to parse _fusion_sources values from JSON-like or Python-literal strings and extract candidate gold IDs for evaluation.

The run added gold-prefix detection and a build_eval_view routine that expands fused clusters to eval_id rows, falls back to cluster _id when no mapping is found, sorts by _fusion_confidence when available, and deduplicates by eval_id.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 69.46%; evaluation attempt counter is 1.
This run was a refinement of the existing evaluation pipeline.
The pipeline added lightweight normalization for postal codes, phone_e164, and phone_raw before matching and fusion.
Blocking changed from TokenBlocker on name_norm alone to StandardBlocker on the combined fields name_norm and city for all dataset pairs.
The matching comparators were reordered and expanded to include postal_code, while house_number tolerance was tightened from a max_difference of 2 to 1.
Matcher weights changed from [0.5, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1] to [0.45, 0.15, 0.15, 0.1, 0.1, 0.05] and the threshold increased from 0.7 to 0.75.

Fusion was revised by adding fusers for id and source and by changing several attribute fusers, including moving name_norm, map_url, phone_raw, phone_e164, and postal_code to shortest_string and moving city, state, country, source, and rating to voting.
The run shifted matching toward stricter, more precise candidate generation by requiring agreement on both name_norm and city during blocking.
The run shifted entity scoring away from a name-dominant five-comparator setup toward a six-comparator setup that explicitly considers postal_code and tighter house number agreement.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was a refinement of the evaluation logic in evaluation_refinement mode rather than an initial draft or a repair.

The dominant strategy was to keep attribute-specific evaluators while refining evaluation alignment through eval_id mapping from fusion sources to gold ids and then evaluating the mapped fused view against the gold test set.
Name, normalized name, website, map URL, phone fields, and most address text fields were evaluated with tokenized matching.
House number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating count were evaluated with numeric tolerance matching.
Categories and any detected list-like columns not already assigned were evaluated with set equality matching.
The source field was newly added to the registered evaluation functions and was evaluated with tokenized matching.

The run preserved helper logic that parses _fusion_sources, detects a dominant gold id prefix, expands fused rows into eval_id candidates, sorts by _fusion_confidence, and deduplicates by eval_id before evaluation.
A fallback was added for gold datasets that lack an id column by copying _id into id, and the code now raises a ValueError if neither id nor _id is present.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 56.45%; previous logged accuracy was 69.46%; evaluation attempt counter is 2.
This run was an evaluation-driven refinement of the existing pipeline.

The pipeline added broad normalization helpers for null cleaning, text standardization, name blocking keys, street normalization, city/state/country normalization, postal code digit extraction, phone normalization, house number cleaning, and category parsing.
Blocking changed from using ["name_norm", "city"] to using derived normalized keys ["name_block", "city_norm"] across all dataset pairs.

Matching comparators were switched to normalized fields such as city_norm and street_norm, the house number tolerance was relaxed from a max difference of 1 to 2, the name weight increased from 0.45 to 0.50, and the matching threshold was lowered from 0.75 to 0.70.

Fusion fusers were changed for many attributes, including id to shortest_string, source to shortest_string, map_url to longest_string, phone_raw to longest_string, phone_e164 to longest_string, house_number to shortest_string, city/state/country to shortest_string, postal_code to longest_string, latitude/longitude to shortest_string, and rating to maximum.
The run shifted matching toward normalized canonical identity signals, emphasizing cleaned name and city features for both blocking and scoring.
pipeline execution succeeded on attempt 1; fusion_data.csv was produced.
This run was an evaluation refinement.
The dominant strategy was to keep the existing ID-aligned evaluation view and field-specific comparators while refining evaluation execution and output handling.
The evaluation continued to use tokenized matching for text fields, numeric tolerance matching for numeric fields, and set equality matching for list-like fields.

Names, addresses, location labels, contact strings, and URLs were grouped under tokenized_match, including name, name_norm, address_line1, address_line2, street, city, state, postal_code, country, phone_raw, phone_e164, website, and map_url.
Numeric-style fields were grouped under numeric_tolerance_match, including house_number, latitude, longitude, rating, and rating_count.
Categories and any detected list-like columns not already registered were grouped under set_equality_match.
The source field was evaluated previously but was removed from the registered evaluation functions in this run.
The import of DataFusionEvaluator was split into a separate import statement without changing evaluation behavior.
The code removed the fallback that created an id column from _id in the gold dataset and the explicit ValueError for missing gold identifiers.
evaluation execution succeeded on attempt 1; pipeline_evaluation.json is available.
overall accuracy is 54.69%; previous logged accuracy was 56.45%; evaluation attempt counter is 3.
